title: Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem
summary: For a lattice band structure with isolated Weyl crossings, the sum of all node charges over the Brillouin zone is zero, forbidding a net chirality in a strictly lattice-regularized system.
assumptions: Only finitely many isolated bad points occur.
regime: Lattice band theory with isolated point nodes.
scope: Lecture One topological and Berry-curvature derivations of chirality cancellation.

source_anchors.notes: The sum rule is proved by degree theory and Stokes's theorem.
The same theorem is recovered from Berry curvature.

symmetries: Brillouin-zone compactness is the global symmetry datum that matters here.
representation: Global topological constraint on a lattice node set.

math_expressions.label: wnn
math_expressions.context: Degree-theory form of the node charge.

math_expressions.label: cl
math_expressions.context: Stokes-theorem derivation of zero net winding number.

math_expressions.label: nna
math_expressions.context: Berry-curvature form of the Nielsen-Ninomiya cancellation theorem.

symbols.meaning: topological charge of the isolated node enclosed by the sphere S_alpha
normalized volume two-form on the target sphere of the map \varphi

failure_modes: The simple statement changes for non-isolated crossings or for continuum models without lattice compactness.
retrieval_hints: Use for chirality cancellation, fermion doubling, and lattice no-go queries.
